zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir William de Houghton於1352年出生在英國藍卡爾普雷斯頓時，他的父親Adam de Hoghton43歲，母親Phillippa de Venables43歲。他於1394年與Jeanette Radcliffe結婚。他們至少是1個女兒的父母。他去世於1422年在英國藍卡爾薩苗萊斯波利享年70歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir William de Houghton于1352年出生在英国藍卡爾普雷斯頓时，他的父亲Adam de Hoghton43岁，母亲Phillippa de Venables43岁。他于1394年与Jeanette Radcliffe结婚。他们至少是1个女儿的父母。他去世于1422年在英国藍卡爾薩苗萊斯波利享年70岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Houghton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: habitational name from any of numerous places called Houghton or Hoghton. Most of the placenames derive from Old English hōh ‘heel, spur of land’ + tūn ‘farmstead, estate’, though some have different etymologies; for example, Little Houghton in Eccles (Lancashire), which derives from Old English halh ‘nook, corner of land’ + tūn, and Houghton in Bigbury (Devon), which may derive from an Old English personal name Huhha + Old English tūn.

variants.name: Haughton
meanings: English: habitational name from Haughton (Cheshire, Lancashire, Northumberland, Shropshire, Staffordshire), or Haughton le Skerne (Durham). The placenames derive from Old English halh ‘nook, corner of land’ + tūn ‘farmstead, estate’. Compare Houghton .
Irish: adopted for Ó hEacháin ‘descendant of Eachán’, a pet form of the personal name Eachaidh (see Hoey ).
Irish: adopted for Ó hEachtair ‘descendant of Eachtair’, a personal name of uncertain origin. Compare Hoctor .

variants.name: Hufton
meanings: English: habitational name; probably from Stony Houghton in Pleasley (Derbyshire), the name of which derives from Old English hōh ‘heel, spur of land’ + tūn ‘farmstead, estate’. Compare Houghton .

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir William de Houghton was born in 1352, in Preston, Lancashire, England, his father, Adam de Hoghton, was 43 and his mother, Phillippa de Venables, was 43. He married Jeanette Radcliffe in 1394. They were the parents of at least 1 daughter. He died in 1422, in Samlesbury, Lancashire, England, at the age of 70.

ko.lifeSummary: Sir William de Houghton(이)가 1352년 영국 랭카실르 플레스턴에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Adam de Hoghton(은)는 43세였고, 그의 어머니 Phillippa de Venables(은)는 43세였다. 그는 1394년 Jeanette Radcliffe(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1422년 영국 랭카실르 샘레스버이에서 70세의 나이로 사망했다.
